title: rubus vestitus subsp. lasioclados
intro: Rubus vestitus subsp. lasioclados, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan strawberry, is a species of bramble in the rose family native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus vestitus subsp. lasioclados include its potential to treat various health conditions such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
